overview: Gruul Prowess in Pioneer leverages the prowess mechanic to rapidly inflate creature stats, applying immediate pressure while maintaining board control. By cycling cheap threats like Soul-Scar Mage and Emberheart Challenger, the deck generates card advantage and combat damage that scales with each attack. Screaming Nemesis provides crucial early-game board presence and a fight trigger to bypass blockers, ensuring the strategy remains aggressive through Pioneer's typical midrange-heavy environment.
win_conditions: Overwhelm the opponent with combat damage from prowess-boosted creatures
Draw out the opponent's resources using Soul-Scar Mage's combat draw trigger
Clear blockers with Screaming Nemesis's fight ability to enable lethal attacks

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two lands and a turn-one or turn-two creature
Hands containing Soul-Scar Mage or Emberheart Challenger with early land drops
Hands that can establish a prowess trigger by turn two
mulligan_criteria: Hands with zero or one land
Hands lacking early creatures or prowess triggers
Hands stuck on high-cost cards without early acceleration

early_game: Prioritize playing lands and casting one- or two-mana creatures to establish prowess
Attack early to trigger Emberheart Challenger's counter ability and Soul-Scar Mage's draw
Use Screaming Nemesis's enter-the-battlefield fight to remove early blockers or chip damage
mid_game: Push damage with multiple prowess-boosted attackers to pressure the opponent's life total
Sequence Nemesis fights to clear the way for larger threats like Emberheart Challenger
Maintain card advantage by repeatedly attacking with Soul-Scar Mage to draw into answers
late_game: Leverage accumulated +1/+1 counters and prowess buffs to close out the game with wide attacks
Keep pressure constant to prevent control or midrange opponents from stabilizing
Use remaining fight triggers to handle late-game threats while pushing lethal

key_cards.name: Screaming Nemesis
key_cards.notes: Cast on turn two to establish prowess and use the fight trigger to bypass blockers or chip damage before attacking.

key_cards.name: Emberheart Challenger
key_cards.role: Mid-game damage engine and counter accumulator
key_cards.notes: Keep on the board to grow with each combat damage trigger; use its prowess synergy to become a large attacker quickly.

key_cards.name: Soul-Scar Mage
key_cards.role: Card advantage and early prowess trigger
key_cards.notes: Attack repeatedly to draw cards into answers or threats; its low cost ensures early board presence and consistent prowess activation.

sequencing_tips: Always play a prowess creature before casting spells that rely on combat damage or buffs
Use Screaming Nemesis's fight ability before declaring attackers to clear blockers efficiently
Attack with Soul-Scar Mage before playing new threats to draw into synergistic cards
Hold up red mana only when necessary, as prowess relies on unspent mana for other effects

matchups.advice: Prioritize early removal and fight triggers to kill Phoenix before it sets up; attack aggressively to prevent it from stabilizing.
Race the opponent by pushing damage with multiple prowess creatures; use Nemesis fights to handle early threats like Wildgrowth Walker.
Apply relentless pressure to prevent them from casting late-game answers; use Soul-Scar to draw into counterspells or removal.
Race their value engine by attacking with prowess-boosted creatures; use fight triggers to remove their early threats.
